channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Canada's PM Carney indicates a move away from economic reliance on the U.S.
item.description: Canada's close economic ties with the U.S. are now a "weakness," not a strength, Prime Minister Mark Carney says. In a video address to the nation, he outlines plans to expand trade beyond America.
item.content:encoded: Canada's close economic ties with the U.S. are now a "weakness," not a strength, Prime Minister Mark Carney says. In a video address to the nation, he outlines plans to expand trade beyond America.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: As world pushes for a ceasefire in Iran and Lebanon, many Israelis support fighting
item.description: While President Trump presses for an end to wars in Iran and Lebanon, many Israelis believe they should keep fighting.
item.content:encoded: While President Trump presses for an end to wars in Iran and Lebanon, many Israelis believe they should keep fighting.
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: U.S.'s 2nd-biggest water reservoir to get a short-term fix amid Colorado River drought
item.description: The Trump administration announced emergency measures to send water from reservoirs high in the river basin to those downstream in the wake of a historically dry Rocky Mountain winter.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration announced emergency measures to send water from reservoirs high in the river basin to those downstream in the wake of a historically dry Rocky Mountain winter.
item.dc:creator: Alex Hager

item.title: Unwanted guns become tools and jewelry
item.description: A group in North Carolina helps people turn unwanted guns into tools and jewelry. For some, it's a healing process.
item.content:encoded: A group in North Carolina helps people turn unwanted guns into tools and jewelry. For some, it's a healing process.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Williams

item.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO. In letter, describes 15 years of emails
item.description: The 65-year-old Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware products, John Ternus, in September. Cook will remain with the company as executive chairman.
item.content:encoded:
The 65-year-old Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware products, John Ternus, in September. Cook will remain with the company as executive chairman.

(Image credit: Godofredo A. Vásquez)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Activists worry that human rights are not prominent in U.S.-Iran negotiations
item.description: Human rights activists are calling on President Trump to push Iran to reopen the internet, not just the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: Human rights activists are calling on President Trump to push Iran to reopen the internet, not just the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: These robots can figure out how to do a task after watching humans do it
item.description: Swiss scientists have published research showing AI-informed robots can learn how to self correct and teach other robots how to behave. It raises questions of consciousness in artificial intelligence.
item.content:encoded: Swiss scientists have published research showing AI-informed robots can learn how to self correct and teach other robots how to behave. It raises questions of consciousness in artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: A 15-year-old shoplifter changed his course when another teen whispered a message
item.description: As a teenager, Everett Miller went through a rough time and started shoplifting. He recalls how a girl's whispered message kept him from making a terrible mistake.
item.content:encoded: As a teenager, Everett Miller went through a rough time and started shoplifting. He recalls how a girl's whispered message kept him from making a terrible mistake.

item.title: Millions of immigrants are stuck in the immigration line
item.description: The line to get permission to legally work and live in the U.S. is getting longer, frustrating immigrants and putting them at greater risk of deportation.
item.content:encoded: The line to get permission to legally work and live in the U.S. is getting longer, frustrating immigrants and putting them at greater risk of deportation.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Tips on 'How to Start' your life's work from journalist Jodi Kantor
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with New York Times journalist Jodi Kantor about her new book, How to Start.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with New York Times journalist Jodi Kantor about her new book, How to Start.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Dorning
